title: polyalthia submontana subsp. sessiliflora
intro: Polyalthia submontana subsp. sessiliflora is a plant species that belongs to the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. It is a medicinal plant that has been traditionally used in various parts of the world for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Polyalthia submontana subsp. sessiliflora plant are typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
